channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: How much freelancers charge 2026
item.description: 2026 freelance developer rates: exact numbers for $50, $100, and $150/hour — includes hidden costs, tax math, and a Python calculator you can reuse today

item.title: Cut cloud emissions 37% without latency hit
item.description: Cut cloud carbon emissions 37% without latency hit — Redis caching, carbon-aware routing, and CPU scaling in a real B2B API. Results from a 2.1M requests/day system.

item.title: Instrument AI pipelines: what to watch when logs fail
item.description: Cut LLM pipeline failures 75% using OpenTelemetry 1.39 — token metrics, vector drift, and the three spans most teams miss.

item.title: Negotiate remote salary: 3 data points to win
item.description: Negotiate remote salary in 2026 with 3 data points: local CPI, client benchmark, value multiplier. Increase accepted asks 20–30% using this calculator.

item.title: CS degree? Skip it: 5 steps to senior
item.description: Senior engineer without a CS degree? 42% cost savings and 60% latency cuts from real systems — the skills that actually matter in 2026

item.title: When queues help (and when they hurt)
item.description: ls from 0.8% to 0.001%. Ordering guarantee with Message queues they drop — taking the other with it.

item.title: Over-engineering costs that hit in 2026
item.description: Cut session service latency 90% by dropping event sourcing — PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7.2, and 300 lines of code replaced 1,200 lines of CQRS and GraphQL

item.title: Why MCP servers break in production
item.description: Cut MCP server latency 80% with async WebSocket transport — the transport choice, URI parsing traps, and cost savings most docs miss

item.title: Portfolio beats pedigree for remote roles
item.description: Portfolio projects that land remote jobs in 2026 aren’t polished clones—they’re proof-of-work systems with deployed demos, post-mortems, and domain depth. Build a real constraint, not a tutorial.

item.title: Don't shard by tenant early. Do this instead
item.description: Cut multi-tenant SaaS costs 40% by avoiding early sharding. See real latency benchmarks, schema migration times, and AWS bills from Jakarta and Philippine startups in 2026.

item.title: Earned 6× in 18 months: how I priced myself globally
item.description: Earned $22k/month from $3.2k by selling outcomes not hours. Includes audit scripts, Next.js 14.4 stack, and SLA templates used to triple hourly rate in 18 months.

item.title: Tune backends for 5G users in 5 steps
item.description: Cut mobile API latency 76% by reusing sockets and caching — socket reuse ratios, TLS handshakes, and the 5G carrier IP rotation pattern most teams ignore.

item.title: Benchmark Redis vs Memcached under real load
item.description: Cut cloud costs 30% with Memcached vs Redis in 2026 — exact latency benchmarks, connection pool failures, and the cache choice most teams get wrong.

item.title: Which dev platform pays African devs fastest in 2026
item.description: Pick the right remote dev platform in 2026 with real payout latency and take-home pay numbers from Lagos, Nairobi, and Johannesburg — Arc paid 40% faster than Toptal, but Toptal’s top clients paid 70% more per hour.

item.title: Oops! 5 TypeScript strict failures
item.description: Cut production incidents 40% with TypeScript strict mode and runtime validation — the real trade-offs between Zod schemas and TypeScript-only types in 2026.

item.title: Choose the right realtime protocol
item.description: Cut realtime API latency and error rates in 2026: compare WebSockets vs SSE vs long polling with concrete numbers and Safari 17 fixes.

item.title: AI multiplies senior devs — juniors get exposed
item.description: it ratio: 87% - External API calls per seco with AI multiplies senior devs — review a junior engineer’s pull request that added.

item.title: Portfolio mistakes that cost African devs remote jobs
item.description: 68% of remote hiring managers reject portfolios without production artefacts. Build a $5 droplet with observability and a postmortem — the artefacts that actually get you hired remotely from Africa.

item.title: Pass remote tech interviews without LeetCode
item.description: Self-taught devs fail remote interviews by over-grinding LeetCode — real debugging and cost-aware design win roles in 2026. Build systems, not clones; measure latency, not just lines of code.

item.title: $14k mistake: simple code beats fancy stacks
item.description: Cut API latency 66% and AWS costs 63% by removing Redis, Kafka, and over-engineered patterns — the real cost of premature abstraction and how to avoid it.
